privacy_intro: This Privacy Policy describes the policies of Sicily Good di Salvatore Guarneri, Cortile Gonzales 7, Lercara Friddi 90025, Italy, email: portasiciliainfo@gmail.com, phone: 3274522382 on the collection, use and disclosure of your information that we collect when you use our website (https://www.portasicilia.com/) (the "Service"). By accessing or using the Service, you are consenting to the collection, use and disclosure of your information in accordance with this Privacy Policy. If you do not consent to the same, please do not access or use the Service.
privacy_operator_company: Sicily Good di Salvatore Guarneri (sole proprietorship)
privacy_operator_address: Registered office: Cortile Gonzales 7, 90025 Lercara Friddi (PA), Italy
privacy_operator_rea: REA registration: [TO BE FILLED] — Palermo Chamber of Commerce
privacy_operator_no_dpo: Porta Sicilia has not appointed a Data Protection Officer (DPO), as the conditions of Article 37 GDPR do not apply. For any request relating to personal data processing, the data subject may write to info@portasicilia.com.
privacy_scope_text: This Notice applies to the processing of personal data of data subjects who visit the Platform, register as users, book services or purchase products, apply to become Partners, receive commercial communications or interact with the official channels of Porta Sicilia.
privacy_subjects_intro: The personal data processed concerns the following categories of data subjects:
privacy_subjects_visitor: Visitors to the Platform (including unregistered visitors).
privacy_subjects_user: Registered users who book experiences, tours and accommodations or purchase products.
privacy_subjects_host: Hosts / Partners who publish listings on the Platform.
privacy_subjects_applicant: Persons who submit an application to become a Partner.
privacy_subjects_subscriber: Subscribers to the newsletter and recipients of commercial communications.
privacy_categories_intro: The categories of personal data processed vary depending on the relationship with Porta Sicilia. They are as follows:
privacy_categories_identification: Identification data: first and last name, date of birth (where provided), Partner’s business name where applicable, tax code or VAT number (for Partners).
privacy_categories_contact: Contact data: email address, phone number, postal address (for shipping and invoicing).
privacy_categories_account: Account data: user identifier, password hash, OAuth identifiers (Google, Apple), language preferences and profile settings.
privacy_categories_booking: Booking data: booking codes, dates, number of participants, any requests communicated to the Host, preferences.
privacy_categories_payment: Payment-related data: amount, currency, transaction outcome, Stripe transaction identifier. Porta Sicilia does NOT store payment-card data, which is collected and processed directly by Stripe in tokenised form.
privacy_categories_content: User-generated content: reviews, uploaded photos, listings (for Hosts), communications exchanged through the Platform.
privacy_categories_technical: Technical and usage data: IP address, device identifier, browser type and version, operating system, language, referrer URL, pages viewed, session duration.
privacy_categories_marketing: Data relating to commercial communications: newsletter subscription email, open/click events, consent status, subscription and unsubscription dates.
privacy_categories_consent: Cookie consent data: choices made on the CMP banner, date and version of the consent recorded.
privacy_categories_special: Porta Sicilia does not deliberately collect special categories of personal data within the meaning of Article 9 GDPR (health, ethnic origin, religious belief, biometric data, etc.). Should a data subject voluntarily provide such information in a review or communication, they will be asked to remove it.
privacy_sources_direct: Data is collected directly from the data subject (registration, Partner application form, newsletter form, bookings, purchases, communications).
privacy_sources_third_party: A limited part of the data may be received from third parties: (i) Google and Apple, in case of OAuth registration (email, name, possibly avatar); (ii) Stripe, in case of payment (tokenised outcome and transaction identifier); (iii) iCal synchronisation of the Host’s calendar with external platforms (Airbnb, Booking) — limited to availabilities, without personal data of the customer.
privacy_purposes_title: 6. Purposes of the processing and legal bases
privacy_purposes_intro: Personal data is processed for the following purposes and with the corresponding legal bases:
privacy_purposes_account: (a) Creation and management of the user account, authentication, maintenance of account security.
privacy_purposes_account_basis: Legal basis: performance of the Platform service contract — Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR.
privacy_purposes_booking: (b) Execution of bookings for experiences, tours and accommodations, including transactional communications and transmission of data necessary to the Host for service provision.
privacy_purposes_booking_basis: Legal basis: performance of contract — Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR.
privacy_purposes_orders: (c) Direct sale of food products in the Shop section: order management, invoicing, shipping.
privacy_purposes_orders_basis: Legal basis: performance of contract — Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR; tax and accounting obligations — Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR.
privacy_purposes_payment: (d) Payment processing via Stripe, including anti-fraud checks and PSD2/SCA authentication.
privacy_purposes_payment_basis: Legal basis: performance of contract — Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR; PSD2 regulatory obligations — Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR.
privacy_purposes_communication: (e) Service communications (confirmations, changes, reminders, booking updates, support).
privacy_purposes_communication_basis: Legal basis: performance of contract — Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR.
privacy_purposes_security: (f) Platform security, fraud prevention, abuse monitoring, dispute and chargeback management.
privacy_purposes_security_basis: Legal basis: legitimate interest of Porta Sicilia in protecting its systems and customers — Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR.
privacy_purposes_analytics: (g) Anonymised/pseudonymised statistical analysis of Platform usage via Google Analytics 4 in Consent Mode v2 with ads_data_redaction enabled.
privacy_purposes_analytics_basis: Legal basis: consent of the data subject expressed via the CMP — Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR.
privacy_purposes_marketing: (h) Sending of the newsletter and commercial communications on Porta Sicilia’s services and initiatives.
privacy_purposes_marketing_basis: Legal basis: express consent of the data subject — Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR; alternatively, "soft-spam" under Article 130(4) of the Italian Privacy Code for offers of services similar to those already purchased, with the right to free objection in every email.
privacy_purposes_reviews: (i) Publication and moderation of reviews left by users.
privacy_purposes_reviews_basis: Legal basis: performance of contract and legitimate interest in Platform transparency — Art. 6(1)(b) and (f) GDPR; compliance with Article 27-quater of the Italian Consumer Code.
privacy_purposes_dsa: (j) Compliance with transparency and notice-and-action obligations under Regulation (EU) 2022/2065 (DSA).
privacy_purposes_dsa_basis: Legal basis: legal obligation — Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR.
privacy_purposes_legal: (k) Compliance with legal obligations (tax, accounting, anti-money laundering where applicable), response to requests from public authorities, exercise or defence of rights in judicial proceedings.
privacy_purposes_legal_basis: Legal basis: legal obligation — Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR; legitimate interest — Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR for defence of rights.
privacy_modality_text: Processing is carried out using electronic and organisational tools suitable to ensure the confidentiality, integrity and availability of the data. Data is processed by authorised personnel of Porta Sicilia and by suppliers appointed as processors, bound by agreements (DPAs) compliant with Article 28 GDPR.
privacy_retention_intro: Personal data is retained for the time strictly necessary to pursue the purposes for which it was collected, according to the following criteria:
privacy_retention_account: User account data: for the duration of the contractual relationship and up to 24 months after the last activity (the account may be deactivated in case of prolonged inactivity).
privacy_retention_booking: Booking and sale data: 10 years from contract performance for tax and civil-law purposes (Articles 2220 Italian Civil Code and 22 DPR 600/1973).
privacy_retention_invoice: Accounting and tax documents: 10 years from the date of issue.
privacy_retention_marketing: Newsletter and commercial-communication data: until consent is revoked or processing is objected to; sending histories are retained for 24 months for statistical purposes.
privacy_retention_analytics: Analytics data (Google Analytics 4): 14 months for standard properties, in pseudonymised form and with IP anonymised.
privacy_retention_consent: Cookie consent records: 24 months from collection, in accordance with the Italian Garante Decision of 10 June 2021.
privacy_retention_logs: System, access and security logs: 12 months.
privacy_retention_applications: Partner applications not approved: 24 months from the decision not to proceed; approved applications: for the duration of the relationship with the Partner.
privacy_retention_legal_hold: In case of litigation or legitimate interest in defending rights, data may be retained for the time necessary, until the final decision becomes res judicata.
privacy_recipients_intro: Personal data may be communicated to service providers acting as processors under Article 28 GDPR. The current list is as follows:
privacy_recipients_stripe: Stripe Payments Europe Ltd (Ireland) and, for certain features, Stripe, Inc. (United States) — payment processing, fraud prevention, SCA. Privacy notice: https://stripe.com/it/privacy.
privacy_recipients_google: Google Ireland Ltd (Ireland) and Google LLC (United States) — OAuth authentication, Google Analytics 4, Google Tag Manager, Google Cloud Translation (automated listing translation). Privacy notice: https://policies.google.com/privacy.
privacy_recipients_apple: Apple Distribution International Ltd (Ireland) and Apple Inc. (United States) — Sign in with Apple authentication, email relay service. Privacy notice: https://www.apple.com/legal/privacy/.
privacy_recipients_aws: Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL (Luxembourg) — hosting of media files (listing images, profiles, reviews) via Amazon S3, with servers located in the eu-west-2 (London) region. Privacy notice: https://aws.amazon.com/privacy/.
privacy_recipients_uc: Usercentrics GmbH (Germany) — cookie-consent management via the Consent Management Platform. Privacy notice: https://usercentrics.com/privacy-policy/.
privacy_recipients_mailerlite: MailerLite UAB (Lithuania) — newsletter delivery and management of commercial contacts. Privacy notice: https://www.mailerlite.com/legal/privacy-policy.
privacy_recipients_smtp: SMTP providers for sending transactional emails (booking confirmations, password recovery, system notifications), bound by DPAs compliant with Article 28 GDPR.
privacy_recipients_hosts: Hosts / Partners: receive from Porta Sicilia only the data strictly necessary to perform the booked service (name, contacts, any specific needs) and act as autonomous controllers of the processing for the provision of the service.
privacy_recipients_authorities: Judicial authorities, supervisory authorities (Italian Garante, AGCM, AGCOM) and other public authorities, upon reasoned request or by legal obligation.
privacy_recipients_advisors: Professional advisers (lawyers, accountants) bound by professional secrecy, to the extent necessary to fulfil legal obligations or defend rights.
privacy_transfers_title: 10. Transfers of data outside the EEA
privacy_transfers_intro: Some of the providers listed above are based or operate in the United States (Stripe Inc., Google LLC, Apple Inc.). Transfers of personal data to these destinations take place in compliance with Chapter V of the GDPR and in particular:
privacy_transfers_dpf: on the basis of the EU-US Data Privacy Framework (European Commission adequacy decision of 10 July 2023) for certified providers;
privacy_transfers_scc: as an alternative, on the basis of the Standard Contractual Clauses adopted by the European Commission with Decision 2021/914/EU, supplemented by additional technical and organisational measures where necessary (Transfer Impact Assessment).
privacy_transfers_rights: The data subject can obtain a copy of the safeguards adopted by writing to info@portasicilia.com.
privacy_rights_intro: The data subject has the right, under Articles 15-22 GDPR, to:
privacy_rights_access: obtain confirmation of the existence of processing of their personal data and access it (Art. 15 GDPR);
privacy_rights_rectification: obtain rectification of inaccurate data or completion of incomplete data (Art. 16 GDPR);
privacy_rights_erasure: obtain the erasure of the data ("right to be forgotten") in the cases provided for (Art. 17 GDPR);
privacy_rights_portability: receive the data in a structured, commonly used and machine-readable format and transmit it to another controller (Art. 20 GDPR);
privacy_rights_objection: object to processing based on legitimate interest or for direct marketing purposes (Art. 21 GDPR);
privacy_rights_withdraw: withdraw at any time consent previously given, without prejudice to the lawfulness of consent-based processing carried out before the withdrawal (Art. 7(3) GDPR);
privacy_rights_adm: not be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing, including profiling, which produces legal or significantly similar effects (Art. 22 GDPR).
privacy_exercise_text: The data subject may exercise their rights by writing to info@portasicilia.com, attaching a copy of an identity document where necessary for verification. Porta Sicilia will respond within 30 days of receipt of the request, extendable by 60 days in case of complexity, with reasoned communication to the data subject. The exercise of rights is free of charge, except in case of manifestly unfounded or excessive requests (Article 12(5) GDPR).
privacy_complaint_text: The data subject has the right to lodge a complaint with the Italian Data Protection Authority (Garante per la protezione dei dati personali, Piazza Venezia 11, 00187 Rome — https://www.garanteprivacy.it) or with the supervisory authority of their Member State of habitual residence or of the place of the alleged infringement (Article 77 GDPR).
privacy_cookies_text: The use of cookies and similar technologies is governed by the Cookie Policy, which forms an integral part of this Notice. Consent is collected through the Consent Management Platform of Usercentrics GmbH and may be modified at any time from the "Cookie settings" panel available in the site footer.
privacy_marketing_text: Newsletter subscription is based on the data subject’s free, specific, informed and revocable consent, collected at the time of subscription. Every email includes a direct link to unsubscribe. In the case of prior purchases of services/products, Porta Sicilia may send communications relating to similar offers (soft-spam, Article 130(4) of the Italian Privacy Code), with the right to free objection at any time.
privacy_profiling_text: Porta Sicilia does not make decisions based solely on automated processing, including profiling, that produce legal effects or similarly significantly affect the data subject (Article 22 GDPR). Listing ranking algorithms are used in search results, described in the Partner Terms under Article 5 of the P2B Regulation (EU 2019/1150): such algorithms do not produce significant effects on end users and do not constitute profiling under Article 22 GDPR.
privacy_security_text: Porta Sicilia adopts technical and organisational measures appropriate to the risk, including: encryption of data in transit (HTTPS/TLS) and at rest for sensitive data, password hashing with strong algorithms, role-based access controls, principle of least privilege, access and security-event logging, environment segregation, periodic audits, encrypted backups, staff training and vulnerability management. Despite the adoption of such measures, no system can guarantee absolute security; in case of a breach, Porta Sicilia will notify the Garante within 72 hours (Article 33 GDPR) and the data subjects in the cases provided for by Article 34 GDPR.
privacy_minors_text: The Platform is not addressed to minors under 18; registration and purchases are reserved to adults. For the processing of data of minors between 14 and 18 years of age in the context of bookings made by a parent, authorisation by the holder of parental responsibility is required. Processing of data of minors under 14 requires the consent of the holder of parental responsibility pursuant to Article 2-quinquies of the Italian Privacy Code. Where Porta Sicilia becomes aware of processing of minors’ data in the absence of such authorisation, it will proceed to delete it.
privacy_changes_text: This Notice may be updated at any time to adapt it to regulatory changes, new processing methods or service developments. The updated version will be published on this page with indication of the last revision date. Material changes will be communicated to data subjects via email or a notice on the Platform.
privacy_contact_text: For any question, request or clarification regarding the processing of personal data, the data subject may write to info@portasicilia.com or by ordinary mail to: Sicily Good di Salvatore Guarneri, Cortile Gonzales 7, 90025 Lercara Friddi (PA), Italy.
terms_operator_intro: The website www.portasicilia.com (the "Platform" or "Site") is operated by:
terms_operator_company: Sicily Good di Salvatore Guarneri (sole proprietorship).
terms_operator_address: Registered office: Cortile Gonzales 7, 90025 Lercara Friddi (PA), Italy.
terms_operator_rea: REA registration: [TO BE FILLED] — Palermo Chamber of Commerce.
terms_operator_brand: For the purposes of these Terms, the entity identified above is hereinafter referred to as "Porta Sicilia".
terms_defs_intro: In these Terms and Conditions, the following terms have the meanings set out below:
terms_defs_user: "User": the natural person who accesses or uses the Platform to browse, book or purchase the Services or Products.
terms_defs_host: "Host" or "Partner": the professional or non-professional entity that publishes on the Platform experiences, tours, accommodations or products intended for sale or booking.
terms_defs_platform: "Platform": the website www.portasicilia.com together with its related digital services, operated by Porta Sicilia as technological intermediary between Users and Hosts.
terms_defs_service: "Services": the experiences, tours and accommodations bookable through the Platform and provided by Hosts.
terms_defs_product: "Products": the goods, in particular food products, sold directly by Porta Sicilia through the Shop section of the Platform.
terms_defs_booking: "Booking": the booking request for a Service formalised on the Platform by payment of the related Service Fee.
terms_defs_commission: "Service Fee": the amount retained by Porta Sicilia as consideration for its intermediation activity, distinct from any balance due to the Host.
terms_acceptance_text_1: Access to, registration on, or use of the Platform implies full acceptance of these Terms and Conditions, of the Privacy Policy and of the Cookie Policy, all of which form an integral part hereof.
terms_acceptance_text_2: Any additional contracts or specific conditions applicable to particular Services or Products shall, in case of conflict, prevail over the general provisions of these Terms.
terms_acceptance_text_3: By using the Platform or communicating with Porta Sicilia electronically, the User accepts that communications shall take place via email or through the Platform’s tools and that such communications satisfy any written-form requirement provided by law.
terms_age_text: Registration and the placement of Bookings or purchases on the Platform are reserved to persons who are at least 18 years old and have legal capacity to enter into binding contracts under Italian law. The processing of personal data of minors under 14 years of age requires the consent of the holder of parental responsibility pursuant to Article 2-quinquies of Italian Legislative Decree 196/2003 (Privacy Code).
terms_auth_intro: The User may register and log in to the Platform using email and password, or through supported third-party authentication providers.
terms_auth_google: Authentication via Google takes place under the OAuth 2.0 protocol with Google Ireland Ltd. In that case, Google’s privacy notice also applies (https://policies.google.com/privacy).
terms_auth_apple: Authentication via Apple takes place under the Sign in with Apple protocol with Apple Distribution International Ltd. If the User uses the "Hide My Email" feature, transactional communications will be relayed through Apple’s relay service; disabling the relay will prevent the delivery of subsequent communications, including those related to Bookings.
terms_auth_credentials: The User is responsible for the confidentiality of their access credentials and for any activity carried out through their account. The User shall promptly notify Porta Sicilia of any unauthorised or suspicious use of their account.
terms_platform_intro: Porta Sicilia acts as technological intermediary between Users and Hosts for experiences, tours and accommodations, and as direct seller for the Products marketed in the Shop section.
terms_platform_exp_text: For experiences or tours booked through the Platform:
terms_platform_exp_item_1: the Booking starts upon payment of the Service Fee to Porta Sicilia;
terms_platform_exp_item_2: Porta Sicilia forwards the request to the Host for availability check;
terms_platform_exp_item_3: on confirmation, the Host will contact the User within 24 hours to provide operational information and arrange payment of the balance. The contract for provision of the Service is concluded directly between User and Host.
terms_platform_exp_no_confirm: If the Host does not confirm availability, Porta Sicilia shall provide a full refund of the Service Fee paid, ordinarily within 14 business days.
terms_platform_stay_item_1: availability is provided and managed by Hosts, including via synchronisation of external iCal calendars;
terms_platform_stay_item_2: the Booking starts upon payment of the Service Fee to Porta Sicilia;
terms_platform_stay_item_3: Porta Sicilia forwards the request to the Host, who undertakes to contact the User within 24 hours to provide practical details and arrange payment of the balance. The accommodation contract is concluded directly between User and Host.
terms_platform_shop_text: Products purchased in the Shop section are sold directly by Porta Sicilia. The sale contract is concluded with the order confirmation following payment. Specific rules are set out in Sections 9 and 10.
terms_payments_stripe: Payments are processed by Stripe Payments Europe Ltd (Ireland) and, for certain features, by Stripe, Inc. (USA), in their capacity as authorised payment service providers. Porta Sicilia does not collect or store the User’s card details: such data is transmitted directly to Stripe in tokenised form and protected under the PCI-DSS standard.
terms_payments_sca: For transactions subject to Directive (EU) 2015/2366 (PSD2), Strong Customer Authentication (SCA / 3D-Secure) may be required.
terms_payments_transfer: Processing of payment data by Stripe, Inc. in the United States takes place in compliance with the EU-US Data Privacy Framework and the Standard Contractual Clauses adopted by the European Commission. Stripe’s privacy notice is available at https://stripe.com/it/privacy.
terms_payments_methods: The payment methods indicated at the checkout page are accepted, including credit and debit cards of major international networks and, where enabled, digital wallets (Apple Pay, Google Pay).
terms_payments_prices: Prices displayed on the Platform are stated in Euros and include VAT where applicable. Before order completion the following are shown separately and clearly: the price of the Service or Product, the Service Fee due to Porta Sicilia, any balance payable to the Host and shipping costs, where applicable.
terms_payments_invoice: Upon request by the User, Porta Sicilia issues fiscal documentation in respect of the Service Fee or the Product purchased, in accordance with Italian tax law.
terms_cancel_framework: Pursuant to Article 59(1)(n) of Italian Legislative Decree 206/2005 (Consumer Code) and Article 51 of Italian Legislative Decree 79/2011 (Tourism Code), the 14-day right of withdrawal does not apply to contracts for leisure activities (experiences, tours, tourism services) and accommodation contracts where the contract provides for a specific date or period of performance. Once a Booking is confirmed, the User may only cancel in accordance with the policies set out below.
terms_cancel_exp_free: ✓ Free cancellation up to 48 hours before the start of the activity: full refund of the amount paid.
terms_cancel_exp_late: ✗ Cancellation less than 48 hours before the activity: no refund.
terms_cancel_stay_30: ✓ Up to 30 days before check-in: refund of any amounts paid to the Host. The Service Fee paid to Porta Sicilia is non-refundable.
terms_cancel_stay_late: ✗ Less than 30 days before check-in: no refund, save for different terms indicated by the Host on the listing page.
terms_cancel_stay_issues_text: In case of unavailability of the accommodation or material non-conformities attributable to the Host, the User shall be entitled, at their choice, to:
terms_cancel_stay_issues_item_1: a full refund of the amounts paid, including the Service Fee; or
terms_cancel_stay_issues_item_2: an alternative accommodation of equivalent standard, where available.
terms_cancel_refund_text_1: Refunds within Porta Sicilia’s remit shall be issued within 14 business days from confirmation of the cancellation, using the same payment method as the original purchase, unless otherwise agreed in writing.
terms_cancel_refund_text_2: Actual crediting times may vary depending on the payment network used and Stripe’s policies.
terms_food_labeling: Food Products are sold in compliance with Regulation (EU) No 1169/2011 on the provision of food information to consumers. Each Product page indicates: name, list of ingredients, allergens highlighted, nutritional values, net weight, batch, best-before or use-by date, name and address of the producer or packer.
terms_food_shipping: Shipping is carried out via a courier selected by Porta Sicilia. Estimated delivery times are indicated during purchase and run from the order confirmation.
terms_food_delays: Any delivery delays not attributable to Porta Sicilia (force majeure, courier unavailability, weather conditions, errors in the address provided by the User) shall not constitute a breach of contract and shall not give rise to refunds, without prejudice to the mandatory rules protecting consumers.
terms_food_orders: Porta Sicilia reserves the right to cancel incomplete, manifestly erroneous or suspicious orders, promptly notifying the User and fully refunding any amounts already paid.
terms_food_guarantee: Food Products are subject to the legal guarantee of conformity provided for in Articles 128 et seq. of the Italian Consumer Code, within the limits compatible with the nature of the Product.
terms_food_withdrawal_title: 10. Right of withdrawal for food Products
terms_food_withdrawal_text_1: For Products purchased in the Shop section, the consumer User is entitled to withdraw from the contract within 14 days of delivery pursuant to Articles 52 et seq. of the Italian Consumer Code, without providing any reason.
terms_food_withdrawal_exceptions: Pursuant to Article 59(1) of the Italian Consumer Code, the right of withdrawal does not apply to Products that may deteriorate or expire rapidly (let. d), to sealed Products that are not suitable for return for hygiene or health-protection reasons and have been opened after delivery (let. e), or to Products that, after delivery, are by their nature inseparably mixed with other items (let. f). Such exclusions are highlighted, where applicable, on the Product page.
terms_food_withdrawal_procedure: To exercise the right of withdrawal, the User shall notify Porta Sicilia of their decision by email to info@portasicilia.com within 14 days of delivery. Porta Sicilia shall refund the amounts received, including standard delivery costs, within 14 days of receipt of the notice, with the right to suspend the refund until the Product has been returned or evidence of its dispatch has been provided. Direct costs of return are borne by the User.
terms_user_obligations_text: The User shall arrive on time at the place, date and time indicated for the performance of the activity or check-in of the accommodation, and shall provide complete and accurate information at the time of booking.
terms_user_obligations_item_2: delay such as to prevent participation;
terms_user_obligations_note: the Booking shall be deemed used and no refund shall be due, save for any express different indication by the Host.
terms_user_obligations_responsible_use: The User undertakes to use the Platform in compliance with the law and in good faith, refraining from conduct that may impair the security, availability or integrity of the Platform, including unauthorised access attempts, automated scraping, distribution of malware or use of collected data for unauthorised direct marketing.
terms_chargeback_text_1: In case of issues with a Booking or purchase, the User is invited to contact Porta Sicilia in advance at info@portasicilia.com, so as to allow assessment and possible amicable resolution of the dispute.
terms_chargeback_text_2: In any case, the User’s right to invoke chargeback procedures provided by payment networks and applicable law remains unaffected. Porta Sicilia reserves the right to provide the payment institution with the contractual documentation related to the contested Booking.
terms_chargeback_text_3: Every right of the User provided for by consumer-protection legislation is reserved.
terms_liability_intermediary: With respect to Services provided by Hosts (experiences, tours, accommodations), Porta Sicilia acts solely as technological intermediary. The User acknowledges that:
terms_liability_item_1: the contract for provision of the Service is concluded directly with the Host;
terms_liability_item_2: the Host is solely responsible for the proper performance of the booked Service, for compliance with applicable rules and for the safety of premises and activities;
terms_liability_item_3: Porta Sicilia is not liable for any non-performance, cancellation, delay, programme change or non-conformity of the Service attributable to the Host.
terms_liability_limits: To the extent permitted by law, Porta Sicilia shall not be liable for: (i) service issues attributable to Hosts; (ii) interruptions or changes due to events beyond its control; (iii) indirect damages, loss of profit or consequential damages; (iv) force majeure events and fortuitous occurrences within the meaning of Article 1218 of the Italian Civil Code.
terms_liability_carveout: The limitations and exclusions of liability under these Terms do not apply in cases of wilful misconduct or gross negligence, of personal injury or harm to health, of breach of essential obligations, nor in any further case in which limitation is mandatorily excluded by law in favour of the consumer (Articles 33–36 of Italian Legislative Decree 206/2005 and Article 1229 of the Italian Civil Code).
terms_reviews_authenticity: Reviews published on the Platform come exclusively from Users who have actually booked and used the Service or purchased the Product reviewed, in accordance with Article 27-quater of the Italian Consumer Code. Porta Sicilia does not publish reviews from unverified Users and does not pay any consideration, benefit or reward in exchange for reviews.
terms_reviews_rules: Reviews must be truthful, based on personal experience and respectful. Reviews containing personal data of third parties, defamatory, offensive, discriminatory or unlawful content, or content harmful to the reputation of the Host or Porta Sicilia, are prohibited.
terms_reviews_moderation: Porta Sicilia reserves the right to remove, downgrade or refuse to publish reviews that breach this policy, providing reasoned notice to the User concerned, in compliance with Regulation (EU) 2022/2065.
terms_ip_ownership: All intellectual property rights in the Platform — including source code, design, trademarks, logos, editorial texts, databases and any other work — belong to Porta Sicilia or its licensors and are protected by applicable copyright and industrial property laws.
terms_ip_reserved: No licence or right over the Platform is granted to the User, save for the use strictly necessary to enjoy the Services. Copying, reproduction, distribution, modification, decompilation, commercialisation or derivative works are prohibited, except within the limits permitted by mandatory provisions (such as the right of citation).
terms_ip_user_content: Content uploaded by the User to the Platform (texts, images, reviews) remains the property of the User. By uploading, the User grants Porta Sicilia a non-exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide licence, valid for the duration of the content’s presence on the Platform, to use, reproduce, adapt, translate (including by automated translation) and publish such content within the Platform and its promotional channels. The User warrants that they hold all the rights necessary to grant this licence.
terms_ip_third_party: The Platform may contain links to third-party websites. Porta Sicilia does not control and is not responsible for the content, privacy policies or practices of such sites.
terms_dsa_intro: Pursuant to Articles 16 and 20 of Regulation (EU) 2022/2065 (Digital Services Act), Users may report to Porta Sicilia content published on the Platform that they believe to be unlawful.
terms_dsa_procedure: Reports shall be sent to dsa@portasicilia.com and must contain: (i) a sufficiently substantiated explanation of why the content is considered unlawful; (ii) the URL or precise identifier of the content; (iii) the reporter’s name and email address; (iv) a good-faith statement regarding the accuracy of the information provided. Reports shall be handled diligently and without undue delay; the reporting User shall be informed of the outcome.
terms_dsa_appeal: Against any decision by Porta Sicilia to remove or maintain content, suspend or restrict an account, an internal complaint may be lodged within 6 months at dsa@portasicilia.com. The possibility of applying to the competent Digital Services Coordinator (AGCOM in Italy) and to out-of-court dispute-settlement bodies designated under Article 21 DSA remains unaffected.
terms_dsa_authority: Requests from competent authorities may be sent to the same address dsa@portasicilia.com, which constitutes the single point of contact pursuant to Articles 11 and 12 of Regulation (EU) 2022/2065.
terms_cookies_text: The Platform uses technical cookies necessary for the operation of the Site and, subject to the User’s consent, third-party analytics and profiling cookies, including Google Analytics 4 and Google Tag Manager (Google Ireland Ltd / Google LLC). Consent is collected through the consent management platform (CMP) provided by Usercentrics GmbH and may be revoked at any time from the "Cookie settings" panel accessible on every page of the Platform. Further information is available in the Cookie Policy.
terms_translation_prevailing: These Terms and Conditions are drafted in Italian. Translations into English, French, German and Spanish are provided as a courtesy: in case of discrepancy, the Italian version prevails.
terms_translation_auto: The content of listings (descriptions of experiences, accommodations and Products) uploaded by Hosts may be translated automatically via Google Cloud Translation. Automated translations may contain inaccuracies and are not legally binding: in case of doubt, the User is invited to refer to the original-language text provided by the Host or to contact Porta Sicilia.
terms_privacy_text: The processing of the User’s personal data is governed by the Privacy Policy, an integral part of these Terms, available at /privacy-policy. The User warrants that the personal data provided is truthful, accurate and up to date, and undertakes to communicate any variation in good time.
terms_host_text_1: Persons who publish experiences, tours, accommodations or Products on the Platform as Hosts or Partners are subject to specific Partner Terms, accepted at onboarding via the dedicated dashboard.
terms_host_text_2: Such conditions govern in particular: identification and verification of the Partner, commission structure and payout arrangements, obligations regarding uploaded content, tax and sectoral compliance, suspension or removal policies. Pursuant to Article 6 of Regulation (EU) 2022/2065 and Articles 14–17 of Italian Legislative Decree 70/2003, Porta Sicilia acts as hosting provider with respect to content uploaded by Hosts.
terms_termination_text: Porta Sicilia may suspend or terminate, in whole or in part, temporarily or permanently, access to the Platform or to individual Services, in particular in case of breach of these Terms, maintenance needs, legal obligations or decisions of authorities. Termination does not give rise to any right to compensation, without prejudice to the User’s rights on Bookings and Products already purchased. The User may delete their account at any time from the profile settings or by writing to info@portasicilia.com, subject to retention obligations imposed by law.
terms_changes_text: Porta Sicilia may update these Terms for operational, technical, regulatory or security reasons. Changes will be notified to the User with reasonable advance notice via the Platform or email; a User who does not wish to accept the changes may withdraw and cease use. Continued use of the Platform after the changes enter into force constitutes acceptance. The last revision date is shown at the top of this document.
terms_law_text_1: These Terms are governed by Italian law.
terms_law_text_2: Disputes with Users qualifying as consumers under Article 3 of Italian Legislative Decree 206/2005 fall under the exclusive jurisdiction of the court of the consumer’s place of residence or elected domicile, where located within the Italian State (Article 66-bis of Italian Legislative Decree 206/2005). For Users not qualifying as consumers, the exclusive jurisdiction is the court of Palermo.
terms_law_severability: The invalidity or unenforceability of any individual clause shall not affect the validity of the contract as a whole; the clause shall be interpreted or replaced within the limits permitted by law to give effect to the parties’ intent.
terms_odr_text_1: Pursuant to Regulation (EU) No 524/2013, we inform consumer Users resident in the European Union that the European online dispute resolution platform is available at https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr. Porta Sicilia’s email address for the purposes of that platform is info@portasicilia.com.
terms_odr_text_2: Porta Sicilia is not currently bound by any ADR body for the out-of-court settlement of disputes; the User is invited to contact Porta Sicilia in advance at the address above to attempt an amicable resolution.
terms_misc_entire: These Terms, together with the Privacy Policy, the Cookie Policy and any Partner Terms, constitute the entire agreement between the User and Porta Sicilia in relation to use of the Platform.
terms_misc_waiver: Failure to exercise a right or to enforce a provision shall not constitute a waiver thereof, nor preclude the subsequent exercise of such right.
terms_misc_assignment: The User may not assign their rights and obligations under these Terms without Porta Sicilia’s prior written consent. Porta Sicilia may assign these Terms to a successor entity in whole or in part of its business.
terms_misc_affiliate: The Platform may promote third-party products or services under affiliate programmes. Such relationships are disclosed where relevant to allow the User to make an informed choice.
terms_contact_text: For any matter relating to these Terms and Conditions, the User may contact Porta Sicilia at info@portasicilia.com or by ordinary mail at: Sicily Good di Salvatore Guarneri, Cortile Gonzales 7, 90025 Lercara Friddi (PA), Italy.
partner_terms_intro_text_1: These Partner Terms (the "Partner Terms") govern the relationship between Sicily Good di Salvatore Guarneri ("Porta Sicilia"), operator of the platform www.portasicilia.com, and any professional or non-professional entity that publishes on the Platform experiences, tours or accommodations intended for end Users (the "Partner" or "Host").
partner_terms_intro_text_2: These Partner Terms apply jointly with the general Terms and Conditions of the Platform and the Privacy Policy, which form an integral part. In case of conflict, the specific provisions of these Partner Terms shall prevail as between Porta Sicilia and the Partner.
partner_terms_intro_text_3: Where the Partner qualifies as a business user within the meaning of Regulation (EU) 2019/1150 ("P2B Regulation"), these Conditions apply in full to the extent provided by that regulation.
partner_terms_defs_intro: In addition to the definitions in the general Terms and Conditions, the following terms apply for the purposes of these Partner Terms:
partner_terms_defs_partner: "Partner" or "Host": the natural person, sole proprietorship, company or entity that, by registering on the Platform, publishes and manages one or more listings of Services intended for Users.
partner_terms_defs_listing: "Listing": the page published on the Platform by which the Partner describes their Service (experience, tour, accommodation), including description, photos, prices, availability and applicable conditions.
partner_terms_defs_dashboard: "Partner Dashboard": the reserved area of the Site through which the Partner manages listings, bookings, communications with Users, payouts and profile.
partner_terms_defs_commission: "Service Fee": the amount retained by Porta Sicilia as consideration for its intermediation activity, in accordance with Section 6.
partner_terms_identity_text_1: The Partner registers by providing truthful, accurate and up-to-date information, including: personal details or business name, tax code and/or VAT number where applicable, address, contacts, IBAN or Stripe Connect credentials for payouts, identity document.
partner_terms_identity_text_2: The Partner declares to hold all the subjective and objective requirements provided by Italian law to offer the proposed Services, including — where applicable — the correct tax qualification (professional, occasional, non-business hospitality, tourist rental), the regional or national identification code for accommodation establishments (CIN/CIR/IUN), and sectoral licences and authorisations.
partner_terms_identity_text_3: Porta Sicilia reserves the right to verify the accuracy of the information provided at any time, including by requesting supporting documentation, and to suspend or disable the Partner’s listings in the absence of adequate cooperation.
partner_terms_onboarding_text: Activation of the Partner on the Platform is conditional upon full acceptance of these Partner Terms by digital tick during onboarding, completion of the required information and approval by Porta Sicilia. Porta Sicilia may refuse the onboarding request without obligation to state reasons, without prejudice to legally mandatory obligations.
partner_terms_listing_accuracy: The Partner warrants that the information published in their listings (descriptions, photos, prices, availability, conditions) is truthful, accurate, up to date, complete and not misleading. The Partner undertakes not to publish unlawful content, content infringing third-party rights, counterfeit or discriminatory content.
partner_terms_listing_photos: The Partner warrants that they hold all the rights needed for the photos and content uploaded, including copyright and the consent of any persons depicted. Porta Sicilia reserves the right to remove images or content that breaches this provision.
partner_terms_listing_prices: The Partner sets their own prices on the Platform. The final price shown to the User is composed of the Partner’s consideration, the Porta Sicilia Service Fee and any applicable taxes and accessory costs. The Partner remains free to apply different prices on different distribution channels (no mandatory parity clause).
partner_terms_listing_availability: The Partner keeps the availability of their inventory up to date, including via iCal synchronisation with external calendars (Airbnb, Booking, own systems). Overbookings caused by failure to update fall under the exclusive responsibility of the Partner.
partner_terms_listing_changes: Material changes to listings already booked (e.g. variation of price, location, duration, capacity) require the express consent of the User already booked; failing this, the Partner shall provide the Service on the originally agreed terms or proceed with a full refund.
partner_terms_commission_structure: For each confirmed Booking, Porta Sicilia retains a Service Fee determined on a percentage basis or as a flat amount, according to the parameters set out in the information sheet published in the Partner Dashboard and updated from time to time. The Service Fee is owed in respect of the intermediation activity, marketing, technological management of the Platform and User support.
partner_terms_commission_changes: Changes to the Service Fee structure shall be notified to the Partner with at least 15 days’ advance notice pursuant to Article 3 of the P2B Regulation, save where the change is required by law, for security reasons or to combat fraud. A Partner who does not wish to accept the new conditions may withdraw without penalty within the notice period.
partner_terms_commission_transparency: The applicable Service Fee and any balance due to the Partner are shown transparently in the Dashboard before each Booking is accepted.
partner_terms_payouts_text_1: User payments are collected via Stripe Payments Europe Ltd, in compliance with PSD2/SCA requirements. The Service Fee is retained directly by Porta Sicilia; the remaining amounts owed to the Partner are transferred as set out below.
partner_terms_payouts_text_2: Depending on the Partner’s configuration on the Platform, payout may take place: (i) by SEPA transfer to the IBAN provided by the Partner, with the frequency indicated in the Dashboard (typically weekly or monthly); (ii) via a Stripe Connect account held by the Partner; (iii) by direct collection of the balance by the Partner from the User at check-in/arrival, where envisaged by the operational model of the Service.
partner_terms_payouts_taxes: The Partner is solely responsible for the tax obligations arising from their contractual relationship with the User (issuance of tax receipts, invoices, income tax returns, VAT, tourist tax where applicable). Save for mandatory legal obligations, Porta Sicilia does not act as withholding agent for the Partner.
partner_terms_payouts_holds: Porta Sicilia may temporarily withhold amounts owed to the Partner in case of: (i) an active dispute by a User; (ii) ongoing chargeback; (iii) reasoned suspicion of fraud or breach of these Conditions; (iv) reasoned request from a competent authority.
partner_terms_obligations_response_time: The Partner undertakes to respond to Booking requests and User messages within 24 hours of receipt. Systematic delays in responding may result in reduced positioning of the listing or suspension of the account.
partner_terms_obligations_execution: The Partner undertakes to perform the Service in the terms, manner and with the features published in the listing, in compliance with applicable safety, hygiene, sanitary, urban and sectoral regulations.
partner_terms_obligations_safety: For experiences and tours, the Partner shall ensure adequate safety conditions, provide the required equipment in good state, instruct the User on participation arrangements and related risks, and suspend or cancel the activity when conditions may compromise personal safety.
partner_terms_obligations_insurance: The Partner is required to take out and maintain the insurance coverage mandated by law and that reasonably necessary in light of the activity carried out (professional indemnity, accident, any sector-specific covers).
partner_terms_obligations_communication: The Partner communicates with the User exclusively through the channels made available by the Platform or, where necessary, through the contact details provided by the User at confirmation. The Partner shall not use the User’s contact data for purposes other than the management of the Booking, save for separate consent of the User.
partner_terms_compliance_text_1: The Partner undertakes to comply with all rules applicable to their activity, including — by way of non-exhaustive example — those concerning:
partner_terms_compliance_item_1: tourist, accommodation or intermediation activity, including regional and municipal provisions (e.g. national CIN or regional identification code for accommodation establishments);
partner_terms_compliance_item_2: safety of premises and offered activities (installations, fitness for use, fire prevention);
partner_terms_compliance_item_3: consumer rights (Italian Legislative Decree 206/2005), personal data protection (Regulation (EU) 2016/679 and Italian Legislative Decree 196/2003) and anti-money laundering rules where applicable;
partner_terms_compliance_item_4: tax obligations (issuance of fiscal documents, tourist tax, VAT, income tax returns);
partner_terms_compliance_item_5: mandatory communications to authorities (e.g. accommodation reports to the Police, ISTAT statistics).

cookie_policy_intro_text: Cookies are small text files that the websites visited send to the user’s device, where they are stored to be retransmitted to those sites on subsequent visits. Equivalent to cookies are other tracking technologies such as pixels, web beacons and device identifiers. This Cookie Policy also applies to such technologies.
cookie_policy_types_intro: On the website www.portasicilia.com we use the following categories of cookies:
cookie_policy_essential_text: They are necessary for the operation of the Site and do not require the user’s consent under Article 122 of Italian Legislative Decree 196/2003. They include session cookies for authentication, shopping cart management, security, load balancing and storage of consent preferences. They are not used for any further purpose.
cookie_policy_functional_text: They improve user experience by storing preferences such as selected language, region and other display settings. They are installed only with the user’s consent.
cookie_policy_analytics_text: They allow aggregated information about the use of the Site (pages visited, duration, traffic sources) to be collected in order to improve its functionality. We use Google Analytics 4 (Google Ireland Ltd / Google LLC). They are installed only with consent; the IP is anonymised and advertising parameters are obfuscated via Google Consent Mode v2 with ads_data_redaction enabled.
cookie_policy_marketing_text: They are used to measure the effectiveness of advertising campaigns and display personalised content. The Platform currently does not use advertising profiling cookies; should any be activated in the future, explicit consent will be requested through the CMP and this notice will be updated.
cookie_policy_third_parties_intro: Cookies and tracking technologies may also be installed by third parties, acting as independent data controllers. The full list of activated services and related cookies is available in the "Cookie settings" panel of the CMP. Main providers:
cookie_policy_uc_text: Consent management platform (CMP) based in Germany. It stores the user’s cookie choices. Classified as essential technical cookie.
cookie_policy_ga_text: Google Ireland Ltd (Ireland) for the EEA; transfers to Google LLC (USA) take place under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework and the Standard Contractual Clauses. Privacy policy: https://policies.google.com/privacy.
cookie_policy_stripe_text: Stripe Payments Europe Ltd (Ireland) and, for certain features, Stripe, Inc. (USA). Essential technical cookies for fraud prevention and payment processing during checkout. US transfers under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework. Privacy notice: https://stripe.com/it/privacy.
cookie_policy_manage_text: The user can change their consent preferences at any time by clicking the "Cookie settings" link in the footer of every page, or by adjusting the settings of their browser to accept, refuse or delete cookies. Disabling technical cookies may impair the proper functioning of the Site.
cookie_policy_changes_text: This Cookie Policy may be updated at any time to reflect technical, regulatory or operational changes to the Site. The last revision date is shown at the top of the page.
cookie_policy_contact_text: For any question regarding the processing of personal data and the use of cookies, the user may contact Porta Sicilia at info@portasicilia.com.
cancellation_policy_intro_text: Porta Sicilia acts as technological intermediary between Users and Hosts. These Cancellation Policies supplement and summarise what is provided in the Site’s Terms and Conditions, to which reference is made for the full contractual context.
cancellation_policy_framework_note: Pursuant to Article 59(1)(n) of Italian Legislative Decree 206/2005 (Consumer Code) and Article 51 of Italian Legislative Decree 79/2011 (Tourism Code), the 14-day right of withdrawal does not apply to contracts for leisure activities (experiences, tours, tourism services) and accommodation contracts where the contract provides for a specific date or period of performance. Once a Booking is confirmed, the User may only cancel in accordance with the policies set out below.
cancellation_policy_food_note: For food Products purchased in the Shop section, the 14-day right of withdrawal applies from delivery, subject to the exceptions set out in Article 59 of the Italian Consumer Code (perishable Products, sealed Products for hygiene reasons opened after delivery, Products inseparably mixed with other items after delivery).
cancellation_policy_procedure_text: To request a cancellation, the User shall use the functionality available in their personal area or contact Porta Sicilia at info@portasicilia.com, indicating the Booking/order code. Refunds within Porta Sicilia’s remit are issued within 14 business days from confirmation of the cancellation, using the same payment method as the original purchase. Actual crediting times depend on the payment network used and on Stripe’s policies.
cancellation_policy_contact_text: For queries on the application of the cancellation policies, please write to info@portasicilia.com.

seo_faq_title: Frequently Asked Questions About Experiences & Accommodations in Sicily — Porta Sicilia
seo_faq_description: Everything you need to know about booking tourist experiences, food tours, villas and accommodations in Sicily on Porta Sicilia. Payments, cancellations, how it works.
faq_how_it_works_1_question: What is Porta Sicilia and how does it work for booking holidays in Sicily?
faq_how_it_works_1_answer: Porta Sicilia is an online platform for booking authentic tourist experiences and accommodations in Sicily, Italy. It connects travelers directly with local Sicilian hosts — tour guides, villa owners, farmhouse operators and B&B hosts — to discover the island authentically. On portasicilia.com you can search, compare and book food and wine tours, excursions, Sicilian cooking classes, villas with pools, seaside apartments and much more, all with secure online payment.
faq_how_it_works_2_question: How do I book an experience or accommodation on Porta Sicilia?
faq_how_it_works_2_answer: Booking on Porta Sicilia is simple: search for the Sicilian destination you're interested in (for example Taormina, Palermo, Cefalù, the Aeolian Islands), browse available experiences or accommodations, select dates and number of participants, then complete your booking with secure online payment. You'll receive a confirmation email with all the details.
faq_how_it_works_3_question: Do I need an account to book on Porta Sicilia?
faq_how_it_works_3_answer: Yes, to book on Porta Sicilia you need to sign up for free with your Google or Apple account. Registration takes just a few seconds and allows you to manage your bookings, save favorite experiences and receive updates about new tourist activities in Sicily.
faq_how_it_works_4_question: Is Porta Sicilia trustworthy? Who is behind the platform?
faq_how_it_works_4_answer: Porta Sicilia is a registered Italian platform based in Sicily. Every experience and accommodation is verified by our team before publication. Payments are processed through secure gateways with SSL encryption and personal data is handled in compliance with GDPR. All information about hosts, itineraries and conditions is transparent and visible before booking.
faq_how_it_works_5_question: What languages is Porta Sicilia available in?
faq_how_it_works_5_answer: Porta Sicilia is available in Italian and English, to welcome both Italian travelers and international tourists who want to visit Sicily. You can switch languages at any time using the language selector in the website navigation bar.
faq_experiences_1_question: What tourist experiences can I book in Sicily on Porta Sicilia?
faq_experiences_1_answer: On Porta Sicilia you'll find a wide selection of tourist experiences across all of Sicily: food and wine tours with Mount Etna wine tastings and local products, Sicilian cooking classes, boat trips to the Aeolian Islands and Zingaro Nature Reserve, Mount Etna trekking, cultural tours in Palermo, Syracuse, Agrigento and the Val di Noto, visits to historic markets like Vucciria and Ballarò, and outdoor activities such as snorkeling and kayaking. Every experience is curated by expert local Sicilian guides.
faq_experiences_2_question: Are Sicily experiences on Porta Sicilia suitable for families with children?
faq_experiences_2_answer: Many experiences on Porta Sicilia are designed for families with children, such as boat tours, nature reserve visits, cooking classes and family-friendly guided excursions. Each experience listing clearly indicates recommended age groups, any restrictions and whether reduced rates are available for children, teenagers or infants.
faq_experiences_3_question: What happens if there's bad weather on the day of my booked experience in Sicily?
faq_experiences_3_answer: For outdoor activities in Sicily (Mount Etna hikes, boat trips, snorkeling), in case of bad weather the local host will contact you to reschedule the experience to an alternative date. If rescheduling is not possible, you will receive a full refund of the amount paid on Porta Sicilia. The specific conditions are indicated on each experience listing.
faq_experiences_4_question: Can I book a private or exclusive experience for my group in Sicily?
faq_experiences_4_answer: Yes, many experiences on Porta Sicilia offer a private booking option, ideal for couples, families or groups of friends who want an exclusive tour. On the experience detail page you'll find whether private mode is available and any supplement. Among the most popular private experiences are private boat tours, reserved winery tastings and in-home cooking classes.
faq_experiences_5_question: How far in advance should I book a tourist experience in Sicily?
faq_experiences_5_answer: On Porta Sicilia we recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance to ensure availability. During peak season (June-September) and for the most popular experiences like Mount Etna excursions, Aeolian Islands tours and wine tastings, we recommend booking at least one week ahead. Booking in advance on portasicilia.com guarantees you the best price.
faq_accommodations_1_question: What types of holiday accommodations can I rent in Sicily on Porta Sicilia?
faq_accommodations_1_answer: On Porta Sicilia you can book villas with private pools, seaside holiday apartments, restored Sicilian farmhouses (masserie), rural countryside houses, B&Bs in historic city centers and traditional dammusi in Pantelleria. All accommodations are selected by our team for quality, authenticity and location, and are available across all of Sicily: from the Taormina and Cefalù coast to the interior, from the Aeolian Islands to the Val di Noto.
faq_accommodations_2_question: How does check-in work at accommodations booked on Porta Sicilia?
faq_accommodations_2_answer: After booking an accommodation on Porta Sicilia, you'll receive an email with detailed check-in instructions: arrival and departure times, driving directions, access code or host contact for key handover. Procedures vary by property — some offer self check-in with digital locks, others provide direct host welcome on arrival.
faq_accommodations_3_question: Can I bring pets to accommodations in Sicily on Porta Sicilia?
faq_accommodations_3_answer: The pet policy varies for each accommodation on Porta Sicilia. On the detail page of each villa, apartment or B&B you'll find clearly indicated whether pets are allowed, any surcharges and the property rules. We recommend filtering accommodations by 'pets allowed' during your search.
faq_accommodations_4_question: Do accommodations on Porta Sicilia offer extra services like breakfast or airport transfers?
faq_accommodations_4_answer: Many accommodations in Sicily on Porta Sicilia offer additional services: breakfast with typical Sicilian products, transfers from Catania or Palermo airport, daily cleaning, bicycle rental, private chef for dinners and concierge service for booking excursions. Available services are listed on the accommodation page with their respective costs.
faq_accommodations_5_question: What is the minimum stay for a holiday rental in Sicily?
faq_accommodations_5_answer: The minimum stay varies by accommodation and time of year. Generally, villas with pools require a minimum of 7 nights during peak season (July-August) and 3-4 nights the rest of the year. Apartments and B&Bs often have a minimum of 2 nights. Each accommodation on Porta Sicilia clearly indicates the minimum stay on the booking page.
faq_payments_1_question: What payment methods does Porta Sicilia accept for booking in Sicily?
faq_payments_1_answer: On Porta Sicilia you can pay with Visa, Mastercard and American Express credit and debit cards, as well as other secure digital payment methods. All prices are in euros (EUR). Payment is made entirely online at the time of booking, with no need to pay cash on arrival.
faq_payments_2_question: Are payments on Porta Sicilia secure?
faq_payments_2_answer: Yes, all payments on Porta Sicilia are protected by SSL encryption and processed through PCI-DSS certified payment gateways. We never store your credit card details. The platform complies with European GDPR regulations for personal data protection.
faq_payments_3_question: When is payment charged for a booking on Porta Sicilia?
faq_payments_3_answer: For tourist experiences, payment is charged immediately upon booking confirmation on Porta Sicilia. For accommodations, a deposit may be required at the time of booking with the balance due before check-in. Payment terms are always visible before completing your booking.
faq_payments_4_question: Can I pay in dollars or pounds on Porta Sicilia?
faq_payments_4_answer: All prices on Porta Sicilia are listed in euros (EUR), the local currency of Sicily and Italy. If you pay with a card in US dollars, British pounds or another currency, your bank will automatically apply the current exchange rate at the time of the transaction.
faq_payments_5_question: Will I receive a receipt or invoice after booking on Porta Sicilia?
faq_payments_5_answer: Yes, after every booking on Porta Sicilia you'll receive a payment confirmation email with all transaction details: amount, date, description of the experience or accommodation booked. For billing requests with tax identification numbers or VAT, you can contact our customer service.
faq_cancellation_1_question: What is the cancellation policy for bookings on Porta Sicilia?
faq_cancellation_1_answer: The cancellation policy on Porta Sicilia varies based on the experience or accommodation booked. Each booking listing clearly indicates the terms: free cancellation (within a certain number of days), partial penalty cancellation or non-refundable. We recommend always checking the specific conditions before completing your booking on portasicilia.com.
faq_cancellation_2_question: How do I cancel a booking on Porta Sicilia?
faq_cancellation_2_answer: To cancel a booking on Porta Sicilia, log into your account, go to the 'My Bookings' section and select the booking to cancel. The refund will be automatically calculated based on the cancellation policy of the experience or accommodation. You'll receive a cancellation confirmation email with refund details.
faq_cancellation_3_question: How long does a refund take after cancelling on Porta Sicilia?
faq_cancellation_3_answer: After cancelling a booking on Porta Sicilia, the refund is processed within 5-10 business days. Actual crediting times to your credit card or bank account depend on your bank and may take an additional 3-15 business days.
faq_cancellation_4_question: Can I change the date of an experience or accommodation already booked on Porta Sicilia?
faq_cancellation_4_answer: Date changes are subject to host availability. On Porta Sicilia we recommend cancelling the existing booking (checking refund conditions first) and creating a new one for the desired date. This way you'll have certainty of availability and the updated price.
faq_cancellation_5_question: What happens if the host cancels my experience or accommodation in Sicily?
faq_cancellation_5_answer: If the host cancels your booking on Porta Sicilia, you will receive a full 100% refund of the amount paid. Our team will contact you to help you find an equivalent alternative experience or accommodation in Sicily, at no additional cost.
faq_partner_1_question: How can I become a host or partner on Porta Sicilia and offer experiences in Sicily?
faq_partner_1_answer: To become a partner on Porta Sicilia and list your tourist experiences or accommodations in Sicily, register on portasicilia.com as an Owner. Fill in your profile with information about your business and submit your application. Our team will review the proposal and contact you within a few days to complete activation and publish your listings.
faq_partner_2_question: What are the requirements to become a host on Porta Sicilia?
faq_partner_2_answer: To become a host on Porta Sicilia we look for tour operators, licensed guides, property owners and businesses offering authentic Sicilian experiences or quality accommodations. You must have all licenses, authorizations and insurance required by Italian and Sicilian regional regulations. We evaluate each application to ensure high standards for travelers.
faq_partner_3_question: How much does it cost to list experiences or accommodations on Porta Sicilia?
faq_partner_3_answer: Registration and listing publication on Porta Sicilia is completely free. Porta Sicilia charges a commission only on bookings actually completed and paid by travelers. There are no fixed monthly costs or subscriptions. Commission rate details are communicated during the activation process.
faq_partner_4_question: How does the host dashboard work on Porta Sicilia?
faq_partner_4_answer: As a host on Porta Sicilia you have access to a dedicated dashboard where you can: manage your availability calendar, view and confirm incoming bookings, set prices and seasonal rates, update descriptions and photos of your experiences or accommodations, and monitor traveler reviews. The dashboard is accessible from both desktop and mobile browsers.
faq_partner_5_question: Can a host offer both tourist experiences and accommodations on Porta Sicilia?
faq_partner_5_answer: Yes, as a partner on Porta Sicilia you can list both tourist experiences (tours, tastings, excursions, classes) and accommodations (villas, apartments, B&Bs, farmhouses). Each listing is managed separately from the dashboard with its own calendar, rates and conditions. This allows you to diversify your offering and reach more travelers looking for holidays in Sicily.

partner_testimonials_1_quote: Since joining Porta Sicilia our direct bookings have doubled. What I love most is always talking with people who truly know the territory.
partner_testimonials_2_quote: Onboarding was effortless and the support is always there. We feel part of a real project, not just another listing platform.
partner_testimonials_3_quote: Finally a platform that values those who craft authentic experiences. Our calendar is fully booked through September.
partner_faq_q1: How much does it cost to become a partner?
partner_faq_a1: Registration is free. We only apply a commission on confirmed bookings.
partner_faq_a2: Our team reviews each application within 3 working days.
partner_faq_a3: We handle traveler payments securely and credit the net amount to you after the stay or experience is completed.
partner_faq_q4: Can I publish more than one listing?
partner_faq_a4: Yes, you can manage an unlimited number of experiences and stays from the same partner area.
partner_faq_a5: An Italian team is available via email to assist you at every stage, from creating your listing to managing bookings.
partner_faq_q6: Can I already be on other platforms?
partner_faq_a6: Yes, we don't require exclusivity. You can be present on other channels as well.
